name: openEuler Computing SIG
description: The Computing team is a interest group related to the OS pan-computing filed, responsible for basic function software packages, C/C++ libraries, device management and other underlying libraries such as multimedia-related libraries.

name: openEuler Computing SIG Repository List
description: openEuler Computing SIG maintains 71 repositories in the Infrastructure and Tools domain

acceptedAnswer.text: openEuler Computing SIG (Special Interest Group) is responsible for The Computing team is a interest group related to the OS pan-computing filed, responsible for basic function software packages, C/C++ libraries, device management and other underlying libraries such as multimedia-related libraries.. The SIG has 14 core members and maintains 71 repositories in the Infrastructure and Tools domain.

mainEntity.name: How to participate in openEuler Computing SIG?

acceptedAnswer.text: To participate in openEuler Computing SIG: 1) Submit Pull Requests to relevant repositories; 2) Subscribe to the mailing list at dev@openeuler.org; 3) Attend SIG regular meetings, see the openEuler website calendar for details.

mainEntity.name: What repositories does openEuler Computing SIG include?

acceptedAnswer.text: openEuler Computing SIG maintains 71 repositories. Full list at https://www.openeuler.org/en/sig/Computing.

mainEntity.name: Who are the Maintainers of openEuler Computing SIG?

acceptedAnswer.text: openEuler Computing SIG has 5 Maintainers, including: Qingqing Li, Bin Wang, Chao Liu. Maintainers are responsible for final code review and merging.
